2604.20811.title: Diagnosing CFG Interpretation in LLMs
2604.20811.paper_summary: As LLMs are increasingly integrated into agentic systems, they must adhere to dynamically defined, machine-interpretable interfaces. We evaluate LLMs as in-context interpreters: given a novel context-free grammar, can LLMs generate syntactically valid, behaviorally functional, and semantically faithful outputs? We introduce RoboGrid, a framework that disentangles syntax, behavior, and semantics through controlled stress-tests of recursion depth, expression complexity, and surface styles. Our experiments reveal a consistent hierarchical degradation: LLMs often maintain surface syntax but fail to preserve structural semantics. Despite the partial mitigation provided by CoT reasoning, performance collapses under structural density, specifically deep recursion and high branching, with semantic alignment vanishing at extreme depths. Furthermore, "Alien" lexicons reveal that LLMs rely on semantic bootstrapping from keywords rather than pure symbolic induction. These findings pinpoint critical gaps in hierarchical state-tracking required for reliable, grammar-agnostic agents.
2604.20811.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地融入自主系統，它們必須遵循動態定義的、機器可解釋的介面。我們將LLMs評估為上下文解釋器：在給定一個新的上下文無關文法的情況下，LLMs能否生成語法上有效、行為上功能性且語義上忠實的輸出？我們引入RoboGrid，一個通過對遞歸深度、表達複雜性和表面風格進行控制壓力測試來解開語法、行為和語義的框架。我們的實驗揭示了一種一致的層級降解：LLMs經常保持表面語法，但未能保留結構語義。儘管鏈接推理（CoT reasoning）提供了部分緩解，但在結構密度下，特別是深度遞歸和高分支的情況下，性能崩潰，語義對齊在極端深度下消失。此外，“外星人”詞彙顯示LLMs依賴於從關鍵字進行語義自舉，而非純粹的符號歸納。這些發現指出了可靠的、與文法無關的代理所需的層級狀態跟踪中的關鍵缺口。
2604.20811.author: Hanqi Li et.al.
2604.20811.authors: Hanqi Li, Lu Chen, Kai Yu
